items.title: Alby
items.description: Browser extension for Bitcoin Lightning payments and Nostr signing. Provides a non-custodial wallet with seamless web integration via the WebLN standard.

items.title: Braiins Hashpower
items.description: On-demand SHA-256 hashrate marketplace launched by Braiins in January 2026 (public beta). Miners buy and sell hashrate through a live order book without long-term contracts or hardware ownership.

items.title: Braiins Hashrate Autopilot
items.description: Official automation tool from Braiins for managing hashrate bids on the Braiins Hashpower marketplace. Rules-based engine that buys hashrate when price crosses thresholds and auto-rebalances between pools.

items.title: BTCMap
items.description: Open-source project mapping Bitcoin-accepting merchants and locations worldwide. Community-driven data using OpenStreetMap as the base layer.

items.title: Caravan
items.description: Open-source multisig coordinator by Unchained Capital. Web-based tool for building and managing multisig transactions without installing software.

items.title: HashGuard
items.description: DIY Python auto-optimizer for the Braiins Hashpower marketplace. Monitors the order book, detects stalled bids, and automatically adjusts pricing to maintain hashrate flow while minimizing overpayment.

items.title: JoinMarket
items.description: Decentralized CoinJoin marketplace. Uses a maker-taker model where liquidity providers (makers) earn fees for providing inputs to CoinJoin transactions, while privacy seekers (takers) pay for the service.

items.title: LNbits
items.description: An open-source Lightning accounting system that sits on top of a Lightning node. Provides a web interface, wallet system, and extensions for building Lightning-powered applications.

items.title: Nostr
items.description: Notes and Other Stuff Transmitted by Relays — a decentralized protocol for creating censorship-resistant social networks and messaging systems based on cryptographic keypairs.

items.title: Nunchuk
items.description: Mobile-first Bitcoin wallet focused on multisig and collaborative custody. Designed for shared vaults and inheritance planning.

items.title: Nutstash
items.description: Web-based Cashu ecash wallet. Enables users to hold and transfer Chaumian ecash tokens backed by Lightning Network bitcoin.

items.title: PayJoin
items.description: A privacy technique (BIP-78, also known as P2EP — Pay-to-Endpoint) where a payment transaction is collaboratively constructed between sender and receiver. Both parties contribute inputs, breaking the common-input-ownership heuristic used by chain analysts.

items.title: Polar
items.description: A one-click Lightning Network simulator for local development and testing. Runs inside Docker and lets developers spin up private networks with multiple node implementations without touching mainnet.

items.title: RoninDojo
items.description: Privacy-focused Bitcoin node operating system. On-chain only, with integrations for Whirlpool (CoinJoin) and Bisq.

items.title: Samourai Wallet
items.description: Privacy-focused Bitcoin wallet for Android, built around on-chain transaction privacy. The project was the origin of several privacy tools later adopted across the ecosystem.

items.title: Specter Desktop
items.description: Open-source DIY multisig coordinator for Bitcoin. Runs as a desktop application with optional hardware wallet integration.

items.title: Strike
items.description: Consumer-friendly Bitcoin and Lightning payment application. Allows users to buy, sell, and send bitcoin with a focus on simplicity and low fees.

items.title: Wasabi Wallet
items.description: Privacy-focused Bitcoin desktop wallet with built-in CoinJoin coordination by default. Uses the WabiSabi credential protocol for CoinJoin transactions.

items.title: What Privacy Stack Should I Use for Mobile?
items.description: Mobile devices are the weakest link in most bitcoiners' privacy stacks. They broadcast location, run closed-source basebands, and constantly phone home to Apple or Google. This page maps a practical mobile privacy stack from OS to wallet.
